questions.question: What is the fundamental building block of modern digital integrated circuits, which acts as an electrically controlled switch?
options: Resistor
Capacitor
Inductor
Transistor
Diode

questions.question: In modern pipelined processors, which architectural technique is primarily used to reduce the performance penalty associated with conditional branch instructions by speculatively guessing the outcome of a branch before it is fully evaluated?
options: Out-of-order execution
Data forwarding
Instruction caching
Branch prediction
Register renaming

questions.question: After logic synthesis, the 'Place and Route' stage in the digital IC design flow is crucial. What is its primary objective?
options: To convert the Register-Transfer Level (RTL) code into a gate-level netlist.
To verify the functional correctness of the design against its specification.
To physically arrange standard cells on the die and connect them with metal traces according to the netlist.
To perform static timing analysis and ensure all timing constraints are met.
To generate test vectors for manufacturing defect detection.

questions.question: In a Non-Uniform Memory Access (NUMA) architecture, what is the primary characteristic that distinguishes it from a Uniform Memory Access (UMA) architecture?
options: NUMA systems inherently support a larger total memory capacity.
In NUMA, all processors have equal access latency to all parts of the main memory.
NUMA architectures guarantee strict sequential consistency across all memory operations.
In NUMA, a processor's access time to memory varies depending on whether the memory is local to its node or remote.
UMA architectures rely exclusively on private caches, whereas NUMA uses shared caches.

questions.question: In the context of digital IC verification, what is the primary purpose of the Universal Verification Methodology (UVM)?
options: To define the Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) for a custom processor.
To provide a standardized, reusable, and scalable framework for verifying complex System-on-Chip (SoC) designs.
To perform physical layout and routing of digital circuits.
To convert a high-level C++ model into a Register-Transfer Level (RTL) description.
To automatically generate optimized gate-level netlists from RTL code.
